item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My headcanon is that it's feeding on normally present wildlife, but it has expressed* a desire for terrestrial mammalian supplements. Keeping it tractable is a higher priority than preserving the bodily integrity of supernumerary hominids.

*Through means eldritch, if not outright squamous and rugose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, thanks for reading- and for running the numbers on this! This is actually intentional, though I think that may not have come through as clearly as I'd thought it would. (Or even not at all, maybe- I do have to admit I was kind of counting on this going largely unnoticed, haha.) I was hoping that combined with the note at the beginning the incongruency between the justification of "it's statistically safer" and numbers that didn't really work (such as the maximal water depth being far too small, as well,) would give off the impression of something being really wrong rather than reading as an author oversight, but thinking about it now I may not have gone about it the best way. One of my earlier drafts solved this specific problem by mentioning a decrease in marine wildlife around the rig, which in hindsight might have been a good idea to keep- the intended implication here is that this thing is getting a lot more, well, food, than it seems at first glance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has potential, but the math doesn't check out at all.

The amount of oil a deepwater offshore rig produces is highly varied, but generally seems to be in the range of thousands of barrels per day. A barrel contains about 5.6 cubic feet. However, the average human only has a volume of about 2-3 cubic feet. If this thing produces 1,000 barrels of oil per day, then it would have to eat two people every day to increase its yield by 0.1%.

But how deadly is being an offshore oil rig worker? According to OSHA, there are 100 fatalities worldwide per year among oil workers. Now, this page is from 1993, and it's possibly an undercount anyhow. So let's say that it's actually 365 deaths per year, or one per day. In other words, for this one rig to increase its output by a measly 0.1%, it would have to triple the global mortality rate of oil rig workers. I don't think I buy that.

We consume (and hence must extract) oil at a phenomenal rate. Global oil consumption matches by volume that of the entire human population about once every month and a half!

item.title: Re: Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much!! I'm glad you enjoyed this.

item.title: Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded: While "Big industrial thing might be alive" isn't a unique angle, the short length and lack of laborious detail makes this fresh enough to enjoy. A clever use of Foundation politics caps this short work off as something I can enjoy and upvote. Good work, author.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I'm very glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
what a whammy of an ending! i loved this to bits!

1+

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Edit: Seeing as this seems to be holding steady for now, I'm fixing the author post.

Hey all, and thanks for reading! This is my second contribution to the Wiki and my first attempt at posting an SCP. I don't really have too much to say about this one- I wanted to write something short as a rebound from a far longer draft I've been staring at since May, and it was fun trying to compress my terminally long writing into something short and (hopefully) snappy.

This was actually far more extensively researched than probably shows- for better or worse, if the numbers don't add up, there's a reason for that. :]

Huge thanks to Pedagon, etoisle, Phiiota, VoidLady and Placeholder McD for speedy and concrete crit, proof-reading and concept help. Y'all really saved this one.
